text: A projectile is launched with speed 20 m/s at 30В° above the horizontal. Taking g = 10 m/sВІ, its time of flight is:
explanation: Time of flight T = 2uВ·sinОё/g = 2(20)(sin30В°)/10 = 2(20)(0.5)/10 = 20/10 = 2 s. Option (2).

text: A car moving at 36 km/h is brought to rest over a distance of 10 m. The magnitude of its uniform retardation is:
explanation: u = 36 km/h = 36Г—(5/18) = 10 m/s. Using vВІ = uВІ в€’ 2as with v = 0: 0 = 10ВІ в€’ 2a(10) в†’ 100 = 20a в†’ a = 5 m/sВІ. Option (2).

text: A block of mass 2 kg rests on a horizontal surface with coefficient of static friction 0.4. The minimum horizontal force (g = 10 m/sВІ) needed to just start it moving is:
explanation: Limiting static friction = ОјВ·N = ОјВ·mg = 0.4 Г— 2 Г— 10 = 8 N. The applied force must just exceed this, so the minimum required is 8 N. Option (2).

text: A force F = (3Г® + 4Дµ) N acts on a body that undergoes a displacement s = (4Г® + 3Дµ) m. The work done by the force is:
A pump lifts 600 kg of water through a height of 20 m in 40 s. Taking g = 10 m/sВІ, the power delivered by the pump is:
Two bodies of masses 3 kg and 6 kg moving toward each other at 4 m/s and 2 m/s respectively collide and stick together. Their common velocity after collision is:
options: 1 m/s in the direction of the 3 kg body
2 m/s in the direction of the 6 kg body
0.67 m/s in the direction of the 3 kg body
explanation: Take the 3 kg body's direction as positive: p = 3(+4) + 6(в€’2) = 12 в€’ 12 = 0. Total momentum is conserved, so (3+6)v = 0 в†’ v = 0 m/s. Option (1).

text: A solid sphere and a solid cylinder of equal mass and radius roll without slipping down the same incline from rest. Which reaches the bottom first?
explanation: Acceleration down an incline for a rolling body is a = gВ·sinОё/(1 + I/mRВІ). For a sphere I/mRВІ = 2/5 (denominator 1.4); for a cylinder I/mRВІ = 1/2 (denominator 1.5). Smaller denominator в†’ larger acceleration, so the sphere is faster and reaches first. Option (2).

text: The moment of inertia of a uniform disc of mass M and radius R about an axis through its centre and perpendicular to its plane is:
explanation: For a uniform circular disc about the central perpendicular axis, I = ВЅMRВІ. (MRВІ is for a ring, 2/5 MRВІ for a solid sphere.) Option (2).

text: The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of a planet whose mass is 2 times and radius is 2 times that of Earth (g = 9.8 m/sВІ) is:
explanation: g = GM/RВІ. g_planet/g_Earth = (M_p/M_E)/(R_p/R_E)ВІ = 2/2ВІ = 2/4 = 0.5. So g_planet = 0.5 Г— 9.8 = 4.9 m/sВІ. Option (1).

text: The escape velocity from the surface of Earth is 11.2 km/s. The escape velocity from a planet having the same density but twice the radius of Earth is:
explanation: v_e = в€љ(2GM/R) = в€љ(2GВ·(4/3)ПЂRВіПЃ/R) = RВ·в€љ(8ПЂGПЃ/3), so v_e в€ќ R at constant density ПЃ. Doubling R doubles v_e: 2 Г— 11.2 = 22.4 km/s. Option (2).

text: A satellite revolves in a circular orbit of radius r around Earth. If the orbital radius is increased to 4r, its orbital speed becomes:
explanation: Orbital speed v = в€љ(GM/r), so v в€ќ 1/в€љr. When r в†’ 4r, v в†’ в€љ(1/4) = 1/2 of the original. Option (2).

text: A particle moves in a circle of radius 2 m with a constant speed of 4 m/s. Its centripetal acceleration is:
explanation: Centripetal acceleration a = vВІ/r = 4ВІ/2 = 16/2 = 8 m/sВІ. Option (3).

text: A steel wire of length 2 m and cross-sectional area 1 mmВІ is stretched by a force of 100 N. If Young's modulus of steel is 2 Г— 10В№В№ N/mВІ, the elongation produced is:
Water rises to a height of 10 cm in a capillary tube. In a tube of half the radius, the water will rise to a height of:
explanation: Capillary rise h = 2TВ·cosОё/(rПЃg), so h в€ќ 1/r. Halving the radius doubles the height: 2 Г— 10 = 20 cm. Option (3).

text: The de Broglie wavelength associated with a particle is inversely proportional to its momentum. If the momentum of an electron is doubled, its de Broglie wavelength becomes:
explanation: de Broglie wavelength О» = h/p, so О» в€ќ 1/p. Doubling the momentum halves the wavelength. Option (2).

text: Water flows through a horizontal pipe whose cross-sectional area reduces from 10 cmВІ to 5 cmВІ. If the speed in the wider section is 2 m/s, the speed in the narrower section is:
explanation: By the continuity equation Aв‚Ѓvв‚Ѓ = Aв‚‚vв‚‚: vв‚‚ = Aв‚Ѓvв‚Ѓ/Aв‚‚ = (10 Г— 2)/5 = 20/5 = 4 m/s. Option (3).

text: A metal rod of length 1 m at 0 В°C has a coefficient of linear expansion 2 Г— 10вЃ»вЃµ /В°C. Its increase in length when heated to 100 В°C is:
explanation: О”L = LО±О”T = 1 Г— 2Г—10вЃ»вЃµ Г— 100 = 2Г—10вЃ»Ві m = 2 mm. Option (2).

text: How much heat is required to convert 10 g of ice at 0 В°C completely into water at 0 В°C? (Latent heat of fusion of ice = 80 cal/g)
explanation: Q = mL = 10 g Г— 80 cal/g = 800 cal (temperature stays at 0 В°C during the phase change). Option (2).

text: An ideal monatomic gas is heated at constant volume so that its absolute temperature doubles. The internal energy of the gas:
explanation: Internal energy of an ideal gas U = (f/2)nRT в€ќ T. If absolute temperature doubles, U doubles. Option (2).

text: In an isothermal process performed on an ideal gas, the change in internal energy of the gas is:
explanation: Internal energy of an ideal gas depends only on temperature. In an isothermal process T is constant, so О”U = 0 (all heat supplied is converted into work, Q = W). Option (3).

text: The ratio of specific heats (Оі = Cp/Cv) for an ideal diatomic gas (neglecting vibration) is:
explanation: A diatomic gas has f = 5 degrees of freedom. Cv = (5/2)R, Cp = Cv + R = (7/2)R, so Оі = Cp/Cv = (7/2)/(5/2) = 7/5. Option (2).

text: The rms speed of gas molecules is v at temperature T. To double the rms speed, the absolute temperature must be raised to:
explanation: v_rms = в€љ(3RT/M), so v_rms в€ќ в€љT. To double v_rms, T must increase by a factor of 2ВІ = 4, i.e. to 4T. Option (2).

text: A simple pendulum has a time period of 2 s. If its length is increased to 4 times the original, its new time period is:
explanation: T = 2ПЂв€љ(L/g), so T в€ќ в€љL. Increasing L by a factor of 4 multiplies T by в€љ4 = 2: new T = 2 Г— 2 = 4 s. Option (2).

text: A particle executes SHM with amplitude 5 cm and angular frequency 2 rad/s. Its maximum speed is:
explanation: Maximum speed v_max = AП‰ = 0.05 m Г— 2 rad/s = 0.1 m/s. Option (2).

text: A stretched string of length 1 m fixed at both ends vibrates in its fundamental mode. If the wave speed on the string is 200 m/s, the fundamental frequency is:
explanation: Fundamental wavelength О» = 2L = 2 m. Frequency f = v/О» = 200/2 = 100 Hz. Option (2).

text: A source of sound emitting at 500 Hz approaches a stationary observer at 34 m/s. Taking the speed of sound as 340 m/s, the apparent frequency heard is:
explanation: For a source approaching a stationary observer, f' = fВ·v/(v в€’ v_s) = 500 Г— 340/(340 в€’ 34) = 500 Г— 340/306 = 500 Г— 1.111 = 555.6 в‰€ 556 Hz. Option (3).

text: Two point charges of +2 ОјC and в€’2 ОјC are separated by 6 cm in vacuum. The magnitude of the force between them is (k = 9 Г— 10вЃ№ NВ·mВІ/CВІ):
The electric potential at a distance of 9 cm from a point charge of 10 nC is (k = 9 Г— 10вЃ№ NВ·mВІ/CВІ):
A parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 4 ОјF in air. When a dielectric of dielectric constant 5 completely fills the space between the plates, the new capacitance is:
explanation: Inserting a dielectric multiplies capacitance by K: C' = KВ·C = 5 Г— 4 = 20 ОјF. Option (3).

text: An electric dipole of dipole moment 4 Г— 10вЃ»вЃ№ CВ·m is placed in a uniform electric field of 5 Г— 10вЃґ N/C, making an angle of 30В° with the field. The torque acting on the dipole is:
explanation: Torque П„ = pEВ·sinОё = (4Г—10вЃ»вЃ№)(5Г—10вЃґ)(sin30В°) = (4Г—10вЃ»вЃ№)(5Г—10вЃґ)(0.5) = (2Г—10вЃ»вЃґ)(0.5) = 1Г—10вЃ»вЃґ NВ·m. Option (1).

text: A wire carries a current of 2 A when connected to a 6 V battery. The amount of charge passing through it in 5 minutes is:
explanation: Charge Q = IВ·t = 2 A Г— (5 Г— 60) s = 2 Г— 300 = 600 C. Option (3).

text: A cell of emf 2 V and internal resistance 0.5 О© is connected across an external resistance of 3.5 О©. The current drawn from the cell is:
An electric heater rated 220 V, 1000 W is operated at 220 V. The energy it consumes in 2 hours is:
explanation: Energy = Power Г— time = 1000 W Г— 2 h = 2000 Wh = 2 kWh (it operates at its rated voltage, so it draws rated power). Option (2).

text: A straight wire of length 0.5 m carrying a current of 4 A is placed perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field of 0.2 T. The force on the wire is:
explanation: F = BILВ·sinОё = 0.2 Г— 4 Г— 0.5 Г— sin90В° = 0.2 Г— 4 Г— 0.5 Г— 1 = 0.4 N. Option (2).

text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular coil of 100 turns and radius 10 cm carrying a current of 2 A is (Ојв‚Ђ = 4ПЂ Г— 10вЃ»вЃ· TВ·m/A):
explanation: B = Ојв‚ЂNI/(2R) = (4ПЂГ—10вЃ»вЃ· Г— 100 Г— 2)/(2 Г— 0.1) = (4ПЂГ—10вЃ»вЃ· Г— 200)/0.2 = (8ПЂГ—10вЃ»вЃµ)/0.2 = 4ПЂГ—10вЃ»вЃґ T. Option (1).

text: The magnetic flux through a coil changes from 2 Wb to 6 Wb in 0.2 s. The magnitude of the average induced emf is:
In a p-n junction diode, when it is forward biased, the width of the depletion region:
explanation: In forward bias, the external field opposes the junction's built-in potential, lowering the barrier so majority carriers cross the junction. This narrows the depletion region. (In reverse bias it widens.) Option (2).

text: In a series LCR AC circuit at resonance, the impedance of the circuit is:
options: Maximum and equal to в€љ(RВІ + (XL в€’ XC)ВІ)
explanation: At resonance XL = XC, so the reactive part cancels and impedance Z = в€љ(RВІ + (XL в€’ XC)ВІ) = R, which is its minimum value (current is maximum). Option (2).

text: The peak value of an alternating voltage is 311 V. Its rms value is approximately:
explanation: V_rms = Vв‚Ђ/в€љ2 = 311/1.414 в‰€ 220 V (this is the standard Indian mains voltage). Option (1).

text: A converging lens has a focal length of 20 cm. An object is placed 30 cm from the lens. Using the convention where distances measured along the direction of incident light are positive, the image distance is:
explanation: Using 1/v в€’ 1/u = 1/f with u = в€’30 cm, f = +20 cm: 1/v = 1/f + 1/u = 1/20 в€’ 1/30 = (3 в€’ 2)/60 = 1/60 в†’ v = 60 cm (real image on the other side). Option (2).

text: A concave mirror has a radius of curvature of 40 cm. Its focal length is:
explanation: For a spherical mirror, focal length f = R/2 = 40/2 = 20 cm. Option (2).

text: In Young's double-slit experiment, the slit separation is 1 mm and the screen is 1 m away. Light of wavelength 600 nm is used. The fringe width is:
explanation: Fringe width ОІ = О»D/d = (600Г—10вЃ»вЃ№ Г— 1)/(1Г—10вЃ»Ві) = 600Г—10вЃ»вЃ¶ = 0.6Г—10вЃ»Ві m = 0.6 mm. Option (2).

text: Unpolarised light of intensity Iв‚Ђ passes through a single ideal polaroid. The intensity of the transmitted light is:
explanation: When unpolarised light passes through one polaroid, the average of cosВІОё over all angles is ВЅ, so the transmitted intensity is Iв‚Ђ/2. Option (2).

text: The work function of a metal is 2 eV. The threshold wavelength of light that can eject electrons from it is approximately (hc = 1240 eVВ·nm):
explanation: Threshold wavelength О»в‚Ђ = hc/П† = 1240 eVВ·nm / 2 eV = 620 nm. Option (2).

text: In the ground state of the hydrogen atom (Bohr model), the energy of the electron is в€’13.6 eV. The energy of the electron in the second excited state (n = 3) is:
explanation: Eв‚™ = в€’13.6/nВІ eV. The second excited state corresponds to n = 3: Eв‚ѓ = в€’13.6/9 = в€’1.51 eV. Option (3).
